channel.title: NPR Topics: National
channel.description: NPR coverage of national news, U.S. politics, elections, business, arts, culture, health and science, and technology. Subscribe to the NPR Nation RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: WATCH LIVE: Artemis II astronauts have splashed down on Earth
item.description: The four astronauts aboard NASA's Artemis II mission are about to plunge through the atmosphere toward Earth, after a successful visit to the moon.
item.content:encoded: The four astronauts aboard NASA's Artemis II mission are about to plunge through the atmosphere toward Earth, after a successful visit to the moon.
item.dc:creator: Amina Khan

item.title: Monster truck rallies are more popular than ever
item.description: The spectacle of a mammoth, balloon-tired pickup truck hurtling through the air in front of thousands of screaming fans has turned into a multi-million-dollar business. Monster Trucks are bigger than ever, spawning several competing national touring companies and expanding overseas. What the hell's going on here? John Burnett will attend Monster Truck Wars--"America's Wildest Monster Truck Show"--in Henderson, Texas, on March 14, talk to some superfans and profile a driver.
item.content:encoded: The spectacle of a mammoth, balloon-tired pickup truck hurtling through the air in front of thousands of screaming fans has turned into a multi-million-dollar business. Monster Trucks are bigger than ever, spawning several competing national touring companies and expanding overseas. What the hell's going on here? John Burnett will attend Monster Truck Wars--"America's Wildest Monster Truck Show"--in Henderson, Texas, on March 14, talk to some superfans and profile a driver.
item.dc:creator: John Burnett

item.title: Epstein survivors have mixed feelings on Melania Trump's call for hearing in Congress
item.description: The first lady made a public statement on Thursday saying she was not friends with Epstein, and calling for further action in Congress. Survivors of the late sex offender's abuse differ on her proposal.
item.content:encoded:
The first lady made a public statement on Thursday saying she was not friends with Epstein, and calling for further action in Congress. Survivors of the late sex offender's abuse differ on her proposal.

(Image credit: Alex Wong)
item.dc:creator: Ava Berger

item.title: Months after the ICE shootings in Minnesota, a federal probe remains elusive
item.description: Officials in Minnesota have sued the Trump administration, saying federal officials are withholding evidence in the killings of U.S. citizens Alex Pretti and Renee Macklin Good by immigration agents in Minneapolis, as well as the non-fatal shooting of a Venezuelan man.
item.content:encoded:
Officials in Minnesota have sued the Trump administration, saying federal officials are withholding evidence in the killings of U.S. citizens Alex Pretti and Renee Macklin Good by immigration agents in Minneapolis, as well as the non-fatal shooting of a Venezuelan man.

(Image credit: Stephen Maturen)
item.dc:creator: Meg Anderson

item.title: Former US ambassador discusses whether the US is safer after launching the Iran war
item.description: Is the U.S. safer after the Trump administration launched a war in Iran? NPR's Leila Fadel speaks with former U.S. Ambassador Nicholas Burns.
item.content:encoded: Is the U.S. safer after the Trump administration launched a war in Iran? NPR's Leila Fadel speaks with former U.S. Ambassador Nicholas Burns.
item.dc:creator: Leila Fadel

item.title: In rare public statement, Melania Trump denies close ties to Jeffrey Epstein
item.description: First Lady Melania Trump made a rare public statement on Thursday, saying she was not friends with the late sex offender Jeffrey Epstein and wasn't introduced to President Trump by him.
item.content:encoded: First Lady Melania Trump made a rare public statement on Thursday, saying she was not friends with the late sex offender Jeffrey Epstein and wasn't introduced to President Trump by him.
item.dc:creator: Saige Miller

item.title: Stephen Colbert and Marjorie Taylor Greene are OUT. Who is IN? The quiz knows
item.description: If you keep up with secret identities, you'll get at least one question right this week!
item.content:encoded: If you keep up with secret identities, you'll get at least one question right this week!
item.dc:creator: Holly J. Morris

item.title: Women are getting most of the new jobs. What's going on with men?
item.description: Over the past year, the vast majority of new jobs have gone to women. One economist says to help men find work, we need to embrace ways to "make girly jobs appeal to manly men."
item.content:encoded:
Over the past year, the vast majority of new jobs have gone to women. One economist says to help men find work, we need to embrace ways to "make girly jobs appeal to manly men."

(Image credit: melitas)
item.dc:creator: Andrea Hsu

item.title: There's growing disquiet in the military. The Iran war made it worse
item.description: Since Trump began his second term, his administration's use of the armed forces has left a growing number in the military unsettled, according to NPR interviews with service members and advocates.
item.content:encoded: Since Trump began his second term, his administration's use of the armed forces has left a growing number in the military unsettled, according to NPR interviews with service members and advocates.
item.dc:creator: Kat Lonsdorf

item.title: 'It just keeps climbing': How Americans are feeling about rising gas prices
item.description: NPR's Michel Martin road trips from D.C. to Ohio to hear how Americans are feeling about gas prices.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Michel Martin road trips from D.C. to Ohio to hear how Americans are feeling about gas prices.
item.dc:creator: Michel Martin
